title: Daily Source Digest
note: Daily source-balanced news digest — morning update.

stories.title: Alan Greenspan, Influential Fed Chair, Dies At 100
stories.summary:
Alan Greenspan, the former chairman of the Federal Reserve who served for more than 18 years, has died at the age of 100. His wife, NBC News anchor Andrea Mitchell, confirmed that he passed away at home from complications related to Parkinson’s disease.

During his tenure, Greenspan presided over the longest U.S. economic expansion in history at the time and earned acclaim as a central figure in American prosperity. He is widely regarded as one of the most influential bankers in the world for his role in shaping modern monetary policy.

However, his legacy remains complex, as he also shares responsibility for the financial crisis that followed his era of growth. Outlets including Al Jazeera and the BBC have highlighted how his leadership navigated significant economic turbulence, encompassing both booms and busts.

links.headline: Former US Federal Reserve Chairman Alan Greenspan dies at age 100
links.excerpt: Greenspan presided over economic turbulence, including crises and booms, during his 18-year tenure as Fed chairman.

stories.title: Another Trump-Backed Candidate Declares Victory Over The Left In Latin America
stories.summary:
Abelardo De la Espriella, a right-wing lawyer and Trump-endorsed candidate, has won the Colombian presidential election in a narrow victory over leftist Senator Iván Cepeda. Initial results indicate that De la Espriella secured approximately 49.7% of the vote, marking a significant political shift for the nation after four years under far-left leadership.

The Trump administration formally congratulated De la Espriella on Sunday night following the announcement of the preliminary counts. This outcome reflects a broader trend of rightward movement in Latin American politics, with international observers noting the razor-thin margin between the two leading candidates.

Despite the initial projections favoring De la Espriella, his opponent has not yet conceded the race. Senator Cepeda stated that the current tally is neither official nor binding, suggesting that final certification may take additional time as vote counts are finalized and verified across the country.

links.headline: Far-right de la Espriella elected Colombia president: What’s next?
links.excerpt: Election results show Trump-endorsed millionaire Abelardo de la Espriella wins by razor-thin margin.

stories.title: Vance says Iran agreed to allow nuclear inspectors into country; hails 'good foundation' to end war
stories.summary:
Vice President J.D. Vance announced on Monday that Iran has agreed to permit inspectors from the United Nations' nuclear watchdog agency access to its nuclear facilities.

He characterized this development as establishing a "good foundation for a successful final deal" aimed at concluding the conflict that began nearly five months ago.

In conjunction with these diplomatic advances, reports indicate that the United States has partially lifted oil sanctions on Iran. This regulatory adjustment aligns with expectations outlined in a memorandum of understanding and coincides with what officials are describing as encouraging negotiations.

links.headline: US partially lifts Iran oil sanctions amid ‘encouraging’ talks
links.excerpt: The move, expected under the MoU, comes as Vice President JD Vance says there's a 'good foundation' for a final deal.
links.imageAlt: US partially lifts Iran oil sanctions amid ‘encouraging’ talks

stories.title: 2 students in custody after shooting at high school in Philippines kills 3
stories.summary:
Two students are in custody after opening fire at a high school in the central Philippines on Monday, resulting in three deaths and seven injuries. Police confirmed that the suspects were armed with handguns when they targeted fellow students during the incident.

Authorities have indicated that the attack was likely motivated by a personal grudge related to bullying. The investigation is ongoing as officials work to determine the full circumstances surrounding the shooting.

links.headline: Three dead in Philippines high school shooting over bullying 'grudge'
links.excerpt: Police say they believe the attack was motivated by a "grudge" over bullying.

stories.title: Prime Minister Keir Starmer Resigns But Sets His Own Months-Long Timetable To Go
stories.summary:
UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer has resigned, initiating a departure process that will span several months. During his resignation announcement, Starmer appeared visibly emotional at times.

The transition marks the seventh change in UK leadership within the last decade, according to Al Jazeera. The outlet notes ongoing questions regarding who will succeed him and the reasons behind his decision.

links.headline: Why has Keir Starmer resigned as UK prime minister, and who will take over?
links.excerpt: The United Kingdom will have its seventh prime minister in a decade.

stories.title: Oliver Tree's loved ones reveal his final wish as singer's body returns to California after helicopter crash
stories.summary:
The body of singer Oliver Tree has returned to California following a fatal helicopter crash that occurred during his world tour. The 32-year-old artist was preparing for an upcoming performance in Brazil when the incident took place.

Family members have announced the creation of a foundation dedicated to supporting artists, fulfilling Tree's final wish to give back to the creative community after his tragic death.

stories.title: Trump says repairs to algae-plagued Reflecting Pool will begin immediately
stories.summary:
President Donald Trump has announced that repairs to the algae-plagued Reflecting Pool in Washington DC will begin immediately. The iconic landmark, which underwent a multi-million dollar renovation including a $14 million relining project, is currently suffering from peeling paint and significant algae growth.

Trump has attributed part of the damage to vandalism, specifically blaming 'radical left lunatics' for the deterioration. This claim contrasts with reports from Al Jazeera, which detail the pool's transformation from its traditional 'US blue' appearance to a green hue due to algae.

Despite the recent extensive renovations intended to preserve the site, the Reflecting Pool continues to face maintenance challenges. The administration's immediate repair plans aim to address these ongoing issues and restore the landmark's condition.

links.headline: Why Washington’s reflecting pool has gone from ‘US blue’ to algae green
links.excerpt: Donald Trump, who spent $14m relining the iconic pool, claims it has been vandalised by 'radical left lunatics'.

stories.summary:
Sir Keir Starmer has announced a timetable for his resignation as prime minister, according to reports from The Spectator Australia. The announcement marks a significant development in the UK political landscape, with live updates being provided by Sky News.

The publication highlights this breaking news as a major event, suggesting that Starmer's departure will have substantial implications for the country's governance and future direction.

stories.title: What we know about how H5N1 bird flu got here and where it may appear next
stories.summary:
Authorities in Australia are investigating the arrival of H5N1 bird flu after infected birds were discovered in Western Australia. The detection marks a significant shift from earlier concerns that the virus would enter the country from northern regions.

The outbreak is now understood to have traveled across the Southern Ocean via migratory bird pathways. Health officials are currently assessing potential landing sites for other infected birds and monitoring additional coastal areas for further cases.

links.headline: What we know about how H5N1 bird flu got here and where it may appear next
links.excerpt: Authorities were worried about avian influenza coming in from the north, until the virus started moving across the Southern Ocean. Now infected birds have been found in Western Australia. Where else could birds carrying the virus land?
links.imageAlt: What we know about how H5N1 bird flu got here and where it may appear next
links.alignmentBasis: source-default-fallback: no article alignment cues; source prior=Center

stories.title: At my friend’s citizenship ceremony, I was moved by the quiet pride in the faces of new Australians | Shadi Khan Saif
stories.summary:
A few days before Australia’s population surpassed 28 million, a neighbor and cricket mate invited the author to attend his citizenship ceremony. The event marked the culmination of more than four years spent navigating forms, waiting periods, interviews, and occasional setbacks.

The experience highlighted the quiet pride visible on the faces of new Australians as they formalized their belonging. Rather than creating a sense of connection, the ceremony recognized years of steadily building lives within the community.

links.headline: At my friend’s citizenship ceremony, I was moved by the quiet pride in the faces of new Australians
links.excerpt: Belonging didn’t start that day. The event recognised what had already been lived, the years of steadily building lives A few days before Australia’s population ticked over 28 million, a neighbour and regular cricket mate invited me along to his citizenship ceremony. We arrived early, a little unsure of what the morning would hold. For him, it had been a long road. More than four years of forms, waiting, interviews, setbacks and that quiet, stubborn kind of hope you don’t always t
links.imageAlt: At my friend’s citizenship ceremony, I was moved by the quiet pride in the faces of new Australians | Shadi Khan Saif

stories.title: Streeting’s surrender is bad for Britain
stories.summary:
Spectator Australia has published an analysis titled "Streeting’s surrender is bad for Britain," focusing on recent volatility in government bonds and a sharper decline in another asset class. The article suggests that the UK Chancellor’s policy decisions are negatively impacting financial markets.

In a separate piece, the publication explores whether Australia should adopt a consumer-first approach to its energy sector. Three national energy regulatory bodies and the Productivity Commission have released reports recommending significant reforms to prioritize household consumers.

These recommendations aim to reshape how energy is priced and distributed across the country. The convergence of advice from multiple independent economic and regulatory agencies highlights growing pressure on policymakers to address rising costs for households.

stories.title: SpaceX inks compute deal with Reflection AI, an open source AI lab
stories.summary:
SpaceX has entered into a significant compute agreement with Reflection AI, an open source artificial intelligence laboratory. The deal involves substantial financial commitments and access to advanced hardware infrastructure.

Under the terms of the arrangement, Reflection AI will pay $150 million monthly starting July 1, 2026, continuing through 2029. This payment secures immediate access to Nvidia's latest GB300 AI chips and supporting hardware.

The resources are located across SpaceX's Colossus 2 data center facility situated near Memphis, Tennessee.

links.headline: SpaceX inks compute deal with Reflection AI, an open source AI lab
links.excerpt: Reflection AI will pay $150 million a month beginning July 1, 2026 through 2029 for immediate access to Nvidia's latest GB300 AI chips and supporting hardware across SpaceX's Colossus 2 data center near Memphis, Tennessee.
links.imageAlt: SpaceX inks compute deal with Reflection AI, an open source AI lab

stories.title: Here’s how you can reserve a Steam Machine
stories.summary:
Google DeepMind has entered into a partnership with film studio A24 to develop artificial intelligence tools for movie production. The collaboration aims to integrate advanced AI capabilities directly into the filmmaking workflow, potentially streamlining various stages of content creation.

The deal is reported to be non-exclusive, allowing both parties to pursue other partnerships while working together on multiple projects. This strategic move highlights the growing intersection between major technology firms and traditional entertainment studios as they explore new creative applications for generative AI.

stories.title: New York City House primary emerges as key battleground in ‘AI civil war’
stories.summary:
The artificial intelligence industry is directing significant financial resources toward the 2026 midterm elections, aiming to shape the legislative framework governing its emerging technologies. AI-focused Super PACs have raised approximately $100 million this election cycle, with roughly $44 million already deployed across dozens of congressional races nationwide.

New York City’s House primary has emerged as a critical focal point in what some observers are calling an "AI civil war." Nearly half of the total spending by these AI-aligned political action committees has been concentrated in a single Manhattan district race, highlighting the intense competition for influence in this specific battleground.

stories.title: How big tech got its way on Trump’s AI executive order
stories.summary:
Donald Trump reversed his position on a planned executive order regarding artificial intelligence, removing the requirement for government safety reviews of new AI models before their release. The decision came just hours before the scheduled signing event.

This reversal has been interpreted as a significant victory for major technology companies, effectively granting them greater freedom to develop and deploy advanced AI systems without prior regulatory oversight.

stories.title: Online racism is significantly affecting mental health, First Nations people say: ‘It’s like carrying a bully in your pocket’
stories.summary:
Online racism is having a significant impact on the mental health of First Nations Australians, according to new reports and calls for regulatory action. The Australian Human Rights Commission has urged platforms to implement a digital duty of care to stop algorithms from promoting racist content.

Advocates describe the persistent nature of online abuse as akin to carrying a bully in one’s pocket, highlighting how social media feeds are increasingly filled with targeted harassment. This ongoing exposure is taking a severe toll on the psychological well-being of Indigenous communities across the country.

stories.title: Francisco may intensify into a super typhoon within 12 hours — Pagasa
stories.summary:
Typhoon Francisco, known internationally as Mekkhala, is projected to intensify into a super typhoon within the next 12 hours. The Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (Pagasa) issued this warning in its latest bulletin on Monday.

The agency reported that the storm was located approximately 625 kilometers east of Tuguegarao City in Cagayan province at the time of the update. This rapid intensification forecast highlights the growing threat posed by the system as it moves through the Philippine Area of Responsibility.

links.headline: Francisco may intensify into a super typhoon within 12 hours — Pagasa
links.excerpt: MANILA, Philippines — Typhoon Francisco (international name: Mekkhala) may further intensify into a super typhoon over the next 12 hours, said the Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (Pagasa) on Monday. Pagasa issued the warning in its latest typhoon bulletin as Francisco was last located 625 kilometers (km) east of Tuguegarao City, Cagayan. READ:

stories.title: Subpoena for Ateneo president moved to June 26 in drowning probe — PNP
stories.summary:
The Philippine National Police–Criminal Investigation and Detection Group (PNP-CIDG) has rescheduled the appearance of Ateneo de Manila University President Fr. Roberto Yap to June 26.

This adjustment pertains to his scheduled testimony in connection with an ongoing investigation into a fatal drowning incident involving two university basketball players.

The probe focuses on the deaths of Rene Baterbonia and Divine Adili, who drowned during a team training exercise.

links.headline: Subpoena for Ateneo president moved to June 26 in drowning probe — PNP
links.excerpt: MANILA, Philippines — Ateneo de Manila University President Fr. Roberto Yap’s scheduled appearance before the Philippine National Police–Criminal Investigation and Detection Group (PNP–CIDG) has been moved to Friday, June 26. This is in connection with the CIDG’s investigation into the fatal drowning of Ateneo basketball players Rene Baterbonia and Divine Adili during a training exercise
links.imageAlt: Subpoena for Ateneo president moved to June 26 in drowning probe — PNP

stories.title: 10,000 teaching positions still vacant in BARMM– official
stories.summary:
At least 10,000 teaching positions remain vacant across the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM), according to a recent disclosure by a regional official. Baintan Ampatuan, a member of the Bangsamoro Transition Authority, revealed this significant staffing gap during a public address.

The situation is further complicated by reports that approximately 30,000 teachers have been serving for years without formal appointment papers from the Ministry of Basic, Higher, and Technical Education. This lack of official documentation raises concerns regarding the employment status and legal standing of these educators within the region's public school system.

links.headline: 10,000 teaching positions still vacant in BARMM– official
links.excerpt: COTABATO CITY —At least 10,000 teaching positions across the Bangsamoro region need to be filled, while some 30,000 teachers have been serving for years, allegedly without formal appointment papers from the Ministry of Basic, Higher, and Technical Education, a member of the Bangsamoro Transition Authority (BTA) revealed. Member of Parliament Baintan Ampatuan dropped the “bombshell”
links.imageAlt: 10,000 teaching positions still vacant in BARMM– official

stories.title: NUJP slams slay try on Capiz broadcaster, urges swift probe
stories.summary:
The National Union of Journalists of the Philippines (NUJP) has strongly condemned the attempted killing of Jay Lavapiez, a radio broadcaster based in Capiz. The organization is calling on law enforcement authorities to conduct a swift and thorough investigation into the incident.

In its statement, the NUJP urged officials to promptly identify both the perpetrators and the mastermind behind the attack. The group emphasized the need for accountability to ensure justice for the targeted journalist.

links.headline: NUJP slams slay try on Capiz broadcaster, urges swift probe
links.excerpt: MANILA, Philippines — The National Union of Journalists of the Philippines (NUJP) condemned the attempted killing of Capiz-based radio broadcaster Jay Lavapiez, calling on authorities to swiftly identify and hold those behind the attack accountable. “The NUJP urges authorities to promptly identify the perpetrators and the mastermind behind the recent attack,” said the group in

stories.title: Sugarcane killer spreads across Bukidnon
stories.summary:
Sugarcane farmers in Bukidnon and neighboring provinces are on heightened alert as a rapidly spreading infestation of the red-striped soft scale insect threatens to ravage the region’s sugar industry. The Sugar Regulatory Administration reported that RSSI-infested sugarcane areas in Bukidnon increased from 30 hectares in Kitaotao on June 17.

The pest poses a significant risk to local agriculture, prompting urgent monitoring and potential intervention measures by agricultural authorities. Farmers are being urged to remain vigilant as the infestation continues to expand across affected regions.

links.headline: Sugarcane killer spreads across Bukidnon
links.excerpt: MARAMAG, Bukidnon — Sugarcane farmers in Bukidnon and neighboring provinces are on heightened alert as a rapidly spreading infestation of the red-striped soft scale insect (RSSI) threatens to ravage the region’s sugar industry. stories.title: Amazon is testing Alexa+ in India with Hindi support
stories.summary:
Amazon is expanding its new conversational AI assistant, Alexa+, to India with a focus on local language support. The company has begun testing a Hindi-language version of the service within the country.

This move signals Amazon's intent to increase the global footprint of Alexa+ by targeting key international markets. Users in India are currently being invited to participate in early testing phases for the new feature.

links.headline: Amazon is testing Alexa+ in India with Hindi support
links.excerpt: Amazon is planning to increase the footprint of its new conversational AI assistant Alexa+ to India and is inviting users in the country to test out a Hindi-language version.
links.imageAlt: Amazon is testing Alexa+ in India with Hindi support

stories.title: Pokémon Go data trained AI that could assist military drones in war zones
stories.summary:
Location data harvested from the augmented reality game Pokémon Go has been utilized to train artificial intelligence systems capable of recognizing and interpreting physical environments. This technology, originally developed for mapping virtual elements onto real-world spaces, is now being adapted for potential military applications.

The AI models trained on this dataset could assist autonomous drones in navigating complex war zones by identifying structures and terrain features. The adaptation highlights how consumer gaming data can be repurposed for defense technologies, raising questions about the intersection of civilian digital infrastructure and military capabilities.

links.headline: Pokémon Go data trained AI that could assist military drones in war zones

stories.title: MacBook Pro M5 review: serious power, still long battery life
stories.summary:
Apple has released the MacBook Pro M5, establishing a new benchmark for performance while maintaining its reputation for exceptional battery life. The latest laptop introduces more storage options and new chips, offering consumers a wide array of configurations to choose from.

A notable change in this release is the introduction of two-tier specifications that vary depending on the processor selected. This shift means potential buyers must carefully consider their processing needs when making a purchase decision.

links.headline: MacBook Pro M5 review: serious power, still long battery life
links.excerpt: Apple laptop sets new performance bar with more storage, new chips and plenty of options, but now has two-tier specs depending on processor Apple’s Macs have been on a roll this year with the brand new budget MacBook Neo and a faster Mac

stories.title: Apocalypse when? ‘Earth’s Black Box’ to be installed in remote Tasmanian airfield
stories.summary:
A steel structure known as the 'Earth's Black Box' is set to be installed at a remote airfield in Tasmania. The project, spearheaded by the Rouser Lab, aims to document humanity's progression toward climate catastrophe.

The device will serve as a long-term record of environmental changes and human impact on the planet. Its placement in a secluded location underscores the urgency of monitoring ecological shifts over time.

links.headline: Apocalypse when? ‘Earth’s Black Box’ to be installed in remote Tasmanian airfield
links.excerpt: Rouser Lab says the steel structure will record ‘every step’ humanity takes towards climate catastrophe Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email</

stories.title: Klue hack results in data breach at several cybersecurity firms
stories.summary:
A data breach originating from market research firm Klue has compromised the information of several prominent cybersecurity companies. The incident highlights the cascading risks inherent in interconnected digital ecosystems, where a vulnerability at one node can expose multiple partners.

Affected organizations include Huntress, HackerOne, Jamf, Recorded Future, and Tanium. These firms, which specialize in protecting other entities from cyber threats, found their own data stolen as a result of the initial compromise at Klue.

links.headline: Klue hack results in data breach at several cybersecurity firms
links.excerpt: Huntress, HackerOne, Jamf, Recorded Future, and Tanium are among the cybersecurity companies that had data stolen following an earlier breach at market research firm Klue.
links.imageAlt: Klue hack results in data breach at several cybersecurity firms
